key_insight: Compositional structure in trained networks appears only in a narrow, target-dependent sweet spot of depth and connectivity; outside it, optimization tends to produce fractured solutions.
engineering_relevance: For AI systems in solid mechanics and fracture modeling, architecture choice matters as much as sparsity: the right pruning pattern and depth can determine whether the model learns reusable physical primitives or brittle shortcuts.
method: Similarity-based pruning (SP) to recover compositional connectivity patterns
Heuristic depth predictor to estimate where compositionality is most likely to emerge
Theoretical analysis using compositional sparsity, volume-ratio arguments, and feature-interference bounds
evidence_signal: The abstract reports empirical results showing compositionality only in specific sparse networks and at specific depths, with gradient descent otherwise converging to fractured solutions, supported by theoretical explanation.
watch_item: The compositional regime is narrow and depends on which connections remain, so naive sparsity or depth scaling may fail even when overall model size looks appropriate.
paper_title: Compositionality Emerges in a Narrow Depth-Connectivity Regime: Architecture Constraints and Solution Manifolds
